description:
Tests covering the full generational GC lifecycle.

Case 1 — Minor collects young, leaves old: allocate objects A (young), trigger minor GC, verify A in old block. Allocate B (young), trigger minor GC, verify B in old, A still in old unchanged.

Case 2 — new→old pointer survives minor GC: allocate old object O (via major GC cycle to promote it), then allocate young Y pointing to O. Minor GC: Y promoted to old, Y.child still points to O (O not moved).

Case 3 — Dead young objects collected: allocate X (young, no reference kept), trigger minor GC, verify X not in old block.

Case 4 — Major trigger after 10 minors: run 10 minor GCs, verify 11th GC dispatches major. After major: old block contains all live data, young block empty, minor_gc_count = 0.

Case 5 — Major trigger by old occupancy: fill old to 71%, trigger minor GC, verify it escalates to major.

Case 6 — Correctness parity: build a 500-element list via a loop. Result must be identical whether using flat Cheney (baseline), minor-only GC, or minor+major GC.
title: gen-gc.6: Integration tests — generational correctness
